records.ety: [OE. swathen, AS. sweðain. See Swath, n., and cf. Swaddle.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Swathed (swād); p. pr. & vb. n. Swathing.]

defs: To bind with a swathe, band, bandage, or rollers.
quotes: Their children are never swathed or bound about with any thing when they are first born.

defs: A bandage; a band; a swath.
quotes: Wrapped me in above an hundred yards of swathe.
Milk and a swathe, at first, his whole demand.
The solemn glory of the afternoon, with its long swathes of light between the far off rows of limes.
